---
title: "Manage Field Settings"
---

---
title: "Manage Field Settings"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Intelligent-Document-Processing-IDP/qnv1765213549503"
section: "Hyland Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)"
---

The Field level settings enable you to configure field validation rules, configure the formatting for field output, configure field type, hide a field from field review screens, mark a field to be ignored for auto extraction and flag a field for review if its confidence is less than the defined threshold values.

## Format Field Output Settings

You can do the following formatting configurations:

- Case Conversion: Instantly switch text to uppercase, lowercase, or sentence case for cleaner outputs.
- Character Removal: Remove unwanted characters like symbols or punctuation while ensuring safe input with built-in validation.
- Truncation Options: Trim field values to your desired length for tighter data control.
- Date Reformatting: Choose from standard date formats and clarify your region’s date convention (month-first or day-first) to ensure accuracy.

For more information, refer to Format Field Output Settings.

## Field Validation

Field validation checks the extracted data for accuracy, completeness, and usausability. You can configure validation rules at field-level.

Validation can be performed internally in IDP config or can be configured to execute a defined process via the IDP connector.

Internal Validation

- Field length
- Min Length: Minimum number of characters allowed
- Max Value: Maximum number of characters allowed
- Regex: Ensures that the field’s value matches the pattern defined by this regular expression
- Required: Indicates mandatory fields.

Process Validation

- Existing Process: Select an existing process template for field validation
- Create Process: Build a script to automate field validation

For more information, refer to Field Validation Settings.

## Override Field Threshold Values

You can define confidence threshold values at global level (project-wide configuration that apply to all extracted fields) and also at individual field-level. Threhold configuration establishes a baseline quality standard so that any field falling below this threshold is automatically flagged for manual review. It also ensures uniform handling of extraction results across the entire project, reducing ambiguity. The global settings acts as the baseline of the threshold standards whereas the field-level settings allow customization.

This feature allows custom thresholds for specific fields to override the global setting. Critical fields such as, Invoice Number, Net Amount and so on can have stricter thresholds (Example: 95%), whereas less critical fields such as, Vendor Address can have relaxed thresholds (Example: 70%).

To override field threshold values:

1. . ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
2. Enter the threshold value (in percentage) for the relevant field.
3. Click Save.
